payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming by children
payload.justification: Claiming represents the strategic process by which children record completion of tasks.

payload.description: Task definition by parents
payload.justification: Task definition is the strategic process parents use to create work items with rewards.

payload.description: Parental review of earnings and completion
payload.justification: Review process enables parents to verify task completion before disbursement.

payload.description: Earnings disbursement to children
payload.justification: Disbursement is the implied payment process following parental review.

payload.description: Pocket money task
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity representing work items with monetary value that children complete and parents define.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Earnings total is a distinct strategic entity representing accumulated monetary value from completed tasks.

payload.description: Monetary reward associated with task
payload.justification: Monetary reward is a defining attribute of pocket money tasks.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Completion status distinguishes available from completed tasks and is central to claiming and review.

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle
payload.justification: The week represents the strategic temporal cycle governing task claiming and earnings totaling.

payload.description: Week end occurrence
payload.justification: Week end is the temporal event when earnings totals become visible to children.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are distinct actors who claim tasks, view availability, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are distinct actors who define tasks and review completion for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to record completed pocket money work
payload.justification: Core strategic goal of empowering children to claim their completed tasks.

payload.description: Provide task and earnings visibility to children
payload.justification: Strategic goal of transparency enabling children to see available tasks and accumulated earnings.

payload.description: Enable parental control of task rewards and payment verification
payload.justification: Strategic goal empowering parents to define incentives and verify work before payment.

payload.description: Claiming pocket money through task completion tracking
payload.justification: Signal SG921 defines the core business process of children claiming pocket money through a tracking mechanism.

payload.description: Parent reporting of child earnings and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG926 defines parent stakeholder reporting capability as a business function for payment purposes.

payload.description: Child visibility restricted to tasks within weekly timeframe
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes a business rule constraining child visibility to weekly timeframe for task entities.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be retained
payload.justification: Signal SG928 specifies the business requirement rule for maintaining historical data.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG922 explicitly establishes tasks as business entities with monetary values that parents can define.

payload.description: Child actor performing tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG923 identifies child actors as entities in the relationship with completed tasks.

payload.description: Monetary value assigned to task
payload.justification: Signal SG922 specifies that tasks have associated monetary values as a defining attribute.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Signals SG923 and SG924 distinguish between completed and available tasks, indicating completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total for child
payload.justification: Signal SG925 defines weekly earnings aggregation as a business data attribute visible to children.

payload.description: Child completes task
payload.justification: Signal SG923 explicitly specifies the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child actors who performed them.

payload.description: Parent defines task
payload.justification: Signal SG922 establishes the relationship between parents and tasks through the definition capability.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG927 establishes the temporal business rule for weekly reset as a recurring cycle.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals SG923 and SG928 reference task completion as a significant business event that triggers relationship recording and historical retention.

payload.description: Child stakeholder
payload.justification: Signals SG921, SG924, and SG925 identify children as primary actors who claim tasks, view availability, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent stakeholder
payload.justification: Signals SG922 and SG926 identify parents as actors who define tasks and review earnings for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to claim pocket money for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG921 defines the core business goal of the pocket money claiming system.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to weekly timeframe
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes a business constraint limiting child visibility requirements to a weekly scope.

payload.description: Historical task data must be preserved
payload.justification: Signal SG928 defines a business constraint requiring retention of historical completion records.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise accountability for establishing and maintaining the parent-child relationship roles through which household responsibility, delegation, and oversight are expressed.

payload.name: Household Work Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to defining compensable household work units, their associated monetary value, completion criteria, and the periodic earnings structure that governs child compensation.

payload.name: Delegation and Oversight Authority
payload.description: Governs the enterprise mechanisms through which authority to assign work is delegated, work commitments are accepted, completion is verified, and approval authority over disbursements is retained by senior stakeholders.

payload.name: Stakeholder Transparency
payload.description: Ensures the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into work obligations, completion status, and earned compensation to participating stakeholders, supporting informed decision-making and accountability.

payload.name: Parental Oversight Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling parental governance over child earning activities, including visibility into performance outcomes and the ability to monitor fulfillment of assigned household obligations.

payload.name: Periodic Opportunity Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing the temporal boundaries of earning opportunities, establishing recurring cycles that define when obligations become available and when eligibility windows reset.

payload.name: Performance Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving the historical record of task fulfillment and earning activity to support accountability, audit, and longitudinal oversight of child participation in the household obligation framework.

payload.name: Earning Opportunity Definition
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing the catalog of household obligations that represent earning potential, defining what constitutes a valid task, the entitlement value associated with each obligation, and the criteria that must be satisfied for an opportunity to be considered fulfilled.

payload.name: Entitlement Claiming Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing the process through which child participants assert completion of assigned obligations and request recognition of earned monetary entitlement, including the accountability framework that determines who may initiate claims and under what triggering conditions.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the child user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the parent user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish household work units eligible for child compensation.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to defining the scope of compensable household work as discrete tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a monetary value for each compensable household work unit.
payload.rationale: Each task entity carries an associated monetary reward attribute that the enterprise must establish and maintain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish completion criteria for household work units.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must define what constitutes a completed task to enable determination of compensation eligibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide aggregate child earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to calculating and presenting total compensation at week-end occurrence, establishing the temporal structure of the earnings obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish mechanisms by which children may claim tasks defined by parents.
payload.rationale: CCIs describe a bidirectional enterprise process: parents define tasks and children claim them. This is a single delegation capability enabling enterprise task assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain oversight of task completion and associated earnings through parental review.
payload.rationale: Parental review of earnings and completion represents an enterprise accountability mechanism for verifying delegated work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall retain authority over the disbursement of earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Disbursement authority is retained at the parental level, representing an enterprise governance constraint aligned with the goal of parental control over rewards and payment verification.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable participating stakeholders to record completed work obligations.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to enable children to record completed pocket money work. At Row 1, this is expressed as the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for stakeholders to record work completion, without specifying system mechanics.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into work obligations and earned compensation to participating stakeholders.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to provide task and earnings visibility. This expresses the enterprise commitment to transparency regarding work obligations and compensation, supporting the stakeholder transparency domain.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to parents a record of each child's earnings.
payload.rationale: HOW-column capability declaration requiring the business to provide parents with visibility into child earnings. Parent stakeholder identified in WHO-column as the recipient of this capability.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to parents a record of each child's completed tasks.
payload.rationale: HOW-column capability declaration requiring the business to provide parents with visibility into task completion outcomes. Parent stakeholder identified in WHO-column as the recipient of this capability.

payload.statement: Parents shall be accountable for defining tasks for children.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column relationship establishing the business responsibility where parents (WHO-column actor) define tasks. Expressed as a business role accountability rather than a system function.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict child participant visibility of work obligations to those available within the current weekly timeframe.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express the same business rule constraining child visibility to weekly boundaries; How-003 frames it as a visibility restriction rule, Why-002 as a policy constraint — both are facets of a single business governance obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall establish a recurring weekly cycle governing the availability of work obligations.
payload.rationale: When-column CCI expressing the business's responsibility to govern temporal availability through a recurring reset cycle — a capability declaration at business level.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a historical record of each task completion.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express the business responsibility to preserve historical task completion data. CCI-ROW2-C-How-004 (How-column) declares the capability requirement; CCI-ROW2-C-Why-003 (Why-column) reinforces the preservation obligation. The single unifying obligation is the business's duty to maintain these records for accountability, audit, and longitudinal oversight purposes.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a catalog of household obligations that represent earning potential for child participants.
payload.rationale: The business must establish and maintain the earning opportunities (tasks) that children can complete to claim pocket money. This is a core business capability derived from the What-column task entity and the Why-column goal of enabling pocket money claims.

payload.statement: The business shall assign a monetary entitlement value to each earning opportunity.
payload.rationale: Each task must have an associated monetary value that defines the earning potential. This is derived from the What-column attribute describing monetary value assignment.

payload.statement: The business shall record the completion status of each earning opportunity.
payload.rationale: The business must maintain records of whether tasks have been completed, which is necessary for the business process of claiming pocket money through task completion tracking.

payload.statement: The business shall account for the weekly earnings total for each child participant.
payload.rationale: The business must maintain accountability for aggregated earnings over the weekly period, supporting the claiming process and financial settlement responsibilities.

payload.statement: A task shall consist of an associated monetary value, a completion status, and obligation criteria.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural composition of the Task entity as a business artefact, capturing the essential attributes that constitute an earning opportunity.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child as a stakeholder who may participate in compensated work activities.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child as a recognised business role with entitlement participation rights; derived from WHO-column actor identification.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the parent as a stakeholder accountable for defining compensable obligations.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent as a recognised business role with task-definition authority; derived from WHO-column actor identification.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child participant's performance of assigned tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to account for task execution by child actors; supports entitlement determination. Derived from WHAT-column entity (child actor performing tasks) and relationship (child completes task).

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each parent-defined task eligible for compensation.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to account for compensable obligations defined by the parent role; supports entitlement governance. Derived from WHAT-column relationship (parent defines task).

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for recognising task completion events that trigger entitlement claiming.
payload.rationale: Business capability to respond to WHEN-column event (task completion) that initiates the claiming process; establishes temporal trigger for entitlement governance.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks throughout the week.
Articulates parental motivation to define tasks with associated monetary rewards.
Expresses the intent to provide children visibility into available and completed tasks.
States the purpose of enabling children to see weekly earnings totals at week end.
Articulates parental need to review child earnings and task completion for disbursement purposes.
Identifies the document section as providing conceptual overview of system purpose and scope.
Defines the core business concept of children claiming pocket money tasks through a tracking mechanism.
Establishes business entities of tasks with associated monetary values that parents can define.
Specifies the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and the child actors who performed them.
Defines child stakeholder visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within a weekly timeframe.
Specifies the business concept of weekly earnings aggregation visible to child stakeholders.
Defines parent stakeholder reporting capability showing child earnings and completed tasks for payment purposes.
Establishes the temporal business rule for weekly task availability reset cycle.
Specifies the business requirement for maintaining historical task completion data.

payload.description: Ambiguous whether this represents audit intent or merely a data retention implementation detail.
